title: Suppress OTel tracing on internal polling fetch calls
summary: Wraps the internal polling fetch call in InngestRun.getRunOutput() with suppressTracing() from @opentelemetry/core to prevent auto-instrumented OTel spans from flooding APM tools (Sentry, Datadog) with hundreds of identical GET spans per workflow run. The actual code change is small (6 additions, 4 deletions in workflows/inngest/src/run.ts plus a new dependency). The bulk of the diff (218+/123-) is pnpm-lock.yaml churn from adding @opentelemetry/core.
maintainerNotes: BUILD IS FAILING: The `suppressTracing()` function from `@opentelemetry/core` is being misused — it expects a `Context` argument, not a callback/lambda. The TypeScript compiler reports: 'Argument of type () => Promise<Response> is not assignable to parameter of type Context' (TS2345), plus cascading errors where .status, .headers, .ok, .text don't exist on Context. The author likely confused this with a different API pattern (e.g., `context.with(suppressTracing(context), fn)`). The approach is sound — suppressing OTel traces on internal polling is a valid fix — but the implementation needs to be corrected. Branch name prefix 'claude/' suggests this was AI-generated code. No tests added. Changeset incorrectly marked as 'minor' — this is a bug fix and should be 'patch'.
